turns.text: Here's a drone shot of my soybean field from last week. Looks good, right?
turns.image_description: Aerial drone image of a soybean field under cloudy daylight, showing uniformly green leaves with no visible spots or discoloration

turns.text: The field appears healthy with uniform green foliage. No signs of stress or disease in this baseline image.
I took another image today. Notice the yellowing in the lower left corner now.
turns.image_description: Same field area 2 days later, with a few isolated yellowing leaves visible in the lower left quadrant under similar lighting conditions

turns.text: The isolated yellowing could indicate early nutrient deficiency or a localized disease. Would you like me to compare with historical data?
Check this close-up of the affected area. There's webbing and small pests on the yellowed leaves now.
turns.image_description: Close-up macro shot of the same field area showing webbing and spider mites on the previously yellowing leaves, with surrounding leaves now showing chlorosis

turns.text: The webbing and spider mites confirm a pest infestation. The chlorosis spreading from the affected leaves suggests active damage. Let me check if thermal imaging would help assess the extent.
Here's the thermal scan. The affected area shows up as hotspots compared to the healthy parts.
turns.image_description: Thermal infrared image of the field showing hotspots corresponding to the affected area, with surrounding healthy plants at normal temperature

turns.text:
What is the primary cause of the observed leaf discoloration and thermal anomalies in the soybean field?

C) Active spider mite infestation causing chlorosis and thermal anomalies
A) Nutrient deficiency
B) Fungal infection
D) Water stress from irrigation issues

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 established baseline health. Turn 2's yellowing suggested early stress (distractor: nutrient deficiency). Turn 3's webbing and spider mites identified pest cause. Turn 4's thermal imaging confirmed active infestation severity.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: Pest identification requires observing progression from initial discoloration to visible webbing and thermal confirmation
